growth_records.medium: Anaerobic sulfate-free basal medium supplemented with casamino acids, 20 amino acids and powdered milk

up.compound: amino acids (mixture; 20 amino acids at 0.1 mM each plus casamino acids 0.05% w/v)
up.method: Depletion of amino acids monitored during pure co-culture growth (GC after 6 M HCl acid hydrolysis and N-pivaloyl iso-propyl ester derivatization; Extended Data Fig. 1b,c); uptake of 13C/15N-labelled amino acid mixture confirmed by NanoSIMS (15N/14N and 12C15N/12C14N imaging of MK-D1 aggregates)

up.compound: ten amino acids/peptides catabolized (2-oxoacid intermediates pyruvate/2-oxobutyrate)

sec.method: Inferred from syntrophic growth requiring H2/formate scavenging partner; NiFe hydrogenase MvhADG-HdrABC; 13C-amino-acid tracing showing transfer to methanogenic partner
Formate dehydrogenase FdhA; interspecies formate transfer; 13C-labelling of partner-derived methane/CO2 varied with methanogenic partner indicating both H2 and formate produced
Oxidative 2-oxoacid decarboxylation releases amino acid carboxylate as CO2; 13C-CO2 measured by isotope analysis (Extended Data Table 2)

sec.compound: methane (CH4, produced by methanogenic partner from MK-D1 H2/formate/CO2)
sec.method: 13C/12C isotope fractionation of methane measured after 120-day incubation (Extended Data Table 2); labelling varied with methanogenic partner

sec.compound: short-chain fatty acids (propionate/butyrate, genome-predicted fermentation by-products)
sec.method: Genome-based metabolic reconstruction; not directly quantified for MK-D1

yields.note: Extremely low cell yield; not enhanced by any of 14 culture conditions tested; no biomass-normalized yields reported (growth tracked only as qPCR 16S rRNA gene copies/ml, ~10^5 at full growth)

growth_records.cite: Imachi, Hiroyuki et al. Isolation of an archaeon at the prokaryote-eukaryote interface. Nature (2020). DOI:10.1038/s41586-019-1916-6. [PMC7015854]
growth_records.method: reported (doubling time 14-25 days from semi-log plot of qPCR 16S rRNA copies); mu calculated from doubling time
growth_records.snippet: The culture consistently had a 30-60-day lag phase and required more than 3 months to reach full growth: around 10^5 16S rRNA gene copies ml-1. The doubling time was estimated to be approximately 14-25 days. ... cultivations were performed
growth_records.notes: Prokaryote = archaeon (Asgard/Lokiarchaeota), qualifies for GrowthDB. Growth quantified only by qPCR of 16S rRNA gene copies (not OD or gDW), so NO uptake/secretion rate is expressible in mmol/gDW/h and none are derivable (no biomass mass, no substrate delta with time given numerically). Growth rate CALCULATED from reported doubling time: mu = ln2/td. td = 14 days = 336 h -> mu = 0.693/336 = 0.00206 /h; td = 25 days = 600 h -> mu = 0.693/600 = 0.001155 /h; hence mu approx 0.00116-0.00206 /h. Doubling time (14-25 d) reported directly, computed by authors from semi-log plot of qPCR data. Medium/conditions from Methods: 20 C, pH 7.5 (adjusted at 25 C), anaerobic N2/CO2 80:20, static (no shaking), dark; inoculum 20% v/v pure co-culture with Methanogenium. Obligate syntroph: growth depends on H2/formate-scavenging partner (Methanogenium or Methanobacterium sp. MO-MB1; SRB Halodesulfovibrio in tri-culture); 10 mM molybdate and 2-bromoethanesulfonate suppressed growth. Substrates catabolized = amino acids (ten catabolized via 2-oxoacid intermediates); products for interspecies transfer = H2, formate, CO2 (partner converts to CH4). Amino acid uptake experimentally verified by depletion monitoring (GC) and by 13C/15N SIP-NanoSIMS. Other tested substrate conditions (casamino acids alone; peptone + powdered milk; 10x and 100x diluted CA+20AA+PM) and temperature series (4-20 C+) did NOT significantly change growth rate/yield (data not shown / reported as no improvement), so a single representative optimal-condition record is emitted. No 13C-MFA flux map, no biomass-specific fluxes, no yields reported.
